what_it_does: Renders a toggle button that switches a live/frozen state, showing a pulsing dot when live and providing accessible labels and titles.
observations: Imports a `stamp` helper from `../lib/address` and spreads it onto the button as data attributes.
Component is a controlled toggle: receives `live` boolean and `setLive` callback.
Provides `aria-label` and `title` that describe current state and the result of tapping.
Inner dot span is `aria-hidden` so only the button is exposed to assistive tech.
standouts: The `stamp('ui://controls/live')` spread is an unusual, file-local instrumentation pattern.
The component is purely presentational and stateless; all state lives outside.
Accessibility text doubles as both control label and state description.
conventions: React functional component with explicit prop type inline.
BEM-style CSS modifier classes driven by props.
concerns: The `stamp` spread's runtime effect is opaque from this file alone.
No visual fallback if CSS classes are missing; relies entirely on external styles.
Prop type for `setLive` accepts any boolean but does not validate caller behavior.
notes: A small, accessible live/freeze toggle that delegates state and styling to its parent and stylesheet.
questions: What does `stamp('ui://controls/live')` return and what consumes it?
Where is the `livedot` CSS defined and what animation handles the pulsing?
Is `setLive` expected to be stable across renders, or could rapid clicks cause stale state?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a live indicator that toggles freeze/resume on tap.
intention_signals: To give users a compact, always-visible control over whether the application streams live updates, with clear affordance and accessibility.
novelty: The `stamp` helper used as a spread for element instrumentation is a distinctive local convention not commonly seen in generic React components.
contribution: A reusable, accessible live/freeze toggle component with explicit state props and instrumentation hooks that a fused system can drop into any surface needing stream control.
summary_for_embedding: LiveDot.tsx exports a controlled React button component that toggles a `live` boolean, applies state-specific BEM classes, spreads a `stamp` data attribute, and exposes accessible labels and titles for a live-stream freeze/resume control.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a small, accessible UI toggle for pausing and resuming a live event stream.
suggested_fields: instrumentation_target: the URI passed to `stamp` and its semantic role
css_dependency: class names that require external stylesheet definitions
proposed_edge_kinds: styles: class names defined in an associated stylesheet
instruments: element tagged by `stamp` for telemetry or tracing

symbols.does: Renders a button that toggles a live/frozen state with accessible labels and a visual dot indicator.
symbols.description: LiveDot is a controlled React functional component that receives `live` and `setLive` props. It renders a `<button>` with BEM-style state classes, spreads a `stamp('ui://controls/live')` result for instrumentation, toggles the boolean on click, and includes an `aria-hidden` inner dot so screen readers perceive only the descriptive button label.
